mnemonics: MUX Is Your Data Selector: Multiplexer Uses X (select) to pick 1 of many inputs
keyPoints: A multiplexer (MUX) selects one of several input lines and routes it to a single output line based on the select signals.
HDL models of combinational circuits like adders use continuous assignments (assign statements) to describe their behavior without sequential logic.
A full adder adds three bits (A, B, Cin) and produces a sum (S) and carry-out (Cout) using the logic: S = A ⊕ B ⊕ Cin, Cout = (A&B) | ((A⊕B)&Cin).
commonMistakes: Confusing the select input order (e.g., thinking select input '00' chooses input 1 instead of input 0).
Using blocking assignments (=) instead of non-blocking (<=) in always blocks for combinational logic, which can create unintended latches.
Forgetting to handle all possible cases in a case statement within an always block, leading to inferred latches instead of pure combinational logic.
